coreCompetencies: Ability to record a ticket with clear reproduction steps and environment details
Basic reading of application logs to locate an error message
Understanding of ticket priority and severity classification
Familiarity with searching a knowledge base for known solutions
Ability to ask the customer the questions needed to narrow a problem
Basic knowledge of how an HTTP request and response work
Understanding of when a ticket must be escalated rather than resolved
Documentation of the steps taken to resolve a ticket
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a ticketing tool such as Zendesk or Jira Service Management
Basic notions of SQL to run a supplied read query
Understanding of the product's main features from a user's point of view
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Resolves routine tickets from the knowledge base
Is learning the product and the support process

coreCompetencies: Reproduction of a reported issue in a test environment from customer input
Reading of logs and stack traces to identify the failing component
Resolution of common API and integration errors such as auth and rate limits
Management of a ticket queue against response and resolution SLAs
Escalation to engineering with a clear, reproducible problem report
Writing of knowledge base articles for issues seen more than once
Use of the product's admin tools to inspect a customer's configuration
Correlation of a customer report with recent releases or incidents
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of reading API responses and status codes for diagnosis
Understanding of basic network troubleshooting such as DNS and TLS errors
Familiarity with querying a database to verify customer data
indicators: Reproduces and resolves common issues independently
Meets SLA targets on an assigned queue
Escalates with reproducible reports

coreCompetencies: Diagnosis of intermittent issues by correlating logs, traces and metrics
Resolution of complex API and integration problems across multiple systems
Isolation of a root cause to a specific service, config or data condition
Ownership of high-severity tickets under tight SLA pressure
Handling of escalations as the bridge between customer and engineering
Maintenance of the knowledge base so repeat issues resolve faster
Reproduction of edge cases that only appear with specific customer data
Guidance of junior engineers on diagnostic technique
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of reading distributed traces across services
Understanding of webhook and event-driven integration failures
Familiarity with scripting to automate a repetitive diagnostic step
indicators: Owns complex and high-severity tickets
Diagnoses root cause across systems
Mentors junior support engineers

coreCompetencies: Leadership of the technical resolution of major customer-facing incidents
Root-cause analysis that identifies recurring failure patterns across tickets
Automation of a repetitive diagnostic or remediation task to save queue time
Design of escalation paths and on-call handoffs with engineering teams
Building of self-service tooling that lets customers resolve common issues
Ownership of the knowledge base structure and its coverage of top issues
Diagnosis of performance and integration problems in customer environments
Feedback of systemic problems into product and engineering backlogs
complementaryCompetencies: Experience building internal diagnostic tools for the support team
Knowledge of observability tooling for tracing customer requests
Understanding of SLA and SLO design for a support function
indicators: Leads resolution of major incidents
Automates repetitive diagnostic work
Feeds systemic issues back to product

coreCompetencies: Leadership of a support engineering team across a product area
Definition of the escalation and incident process between support and engineering
Design of self-service and deflection strategy to reduce ticket volume
Analysis of ticket data to find the systemic causes behind support load
Ownership of SLA and SLO targets and the staffing to meet them
Building of the tooling that gives the team visibility into customer state
Direction of root-cause reviews that turn incidents into product fixes
Development of support engineers through diagnostic and product training
complementaryCompetencies: Experience defining support metrics beyond ticket count and CSAT
Knowledge of building a diagnostic platform for the support organization
Understanding of how release quality drives support volume
indicators: Leads a support engineering team
Owns SLA and SLO targets for an area
Drives incidents into product fixes

coreCompetencies: Direction of the support engineering function across a business area
Definition of the self-service and automation strategy that bends the cost curve
Ownership of the systemic root-cause program that reduces recurring tickets
Design of the tooling and data platform that powers support at scale
Definition of the SLA and SLO framework and how it maps to customer tiers
Alignment of support priorities with product and engineering roadmaps
Establishment of the escalation model across support and engineering on-call
Development of support engineering leaders and diagnostic specialists
complementaryCompetencies: Experience shaping the support tooling and automation investment
Knowledge of support economics and cost-to-serve analysis
Understanding of how support insight informs product reliability work
indicators: Directs support engineering for an area
Owns the automation and self-service strategy
Aligns support with product roadmaps

coreCompetencies: Executive leadership of support engineering across the organization
Definition of the strategy that shifts resolution from tickets to self-service
Ownership of the systemic reliability program driven by support insight
Design of the support data and tooling architecture at company scale
Definition of the SLA and SLO commitments the company makes to customers
Alignment of the support function with product, engineering and reliability
Communication of support-driven reliability priorities to executive leadership
Development of the leadership bench for the support engineering discipline
complementaryCompetencies: Experience representing the voice of the customer to executive leadership
Knowledge of cost-to-serve modeling at organizational scale
Understanding of how support scales without linear headcount growth
indicators: Reports to CEO or CTO on support
Owns support strategy across the company
Drives reliability from support insight

coreCompetencies: Definition of the company thesis on how support scales through engineering
Architecture of the self-service and automation strategy as a structural cost lever
Ownership of the systemic root-cause discipline that shapes product reliability
Design of the support data platform as the company's early-warning system
Definition of the SLA and SLO doctrine that governs customer commitments
Alignment of support-driven insight with product and reliability strategy
Establishment of support engineering as a recognized engineering discipline
Direction of how the function turns customer pain into systemic improvement
complementaryCompetencies: Advisory on support engineering strategy across the industry
Influence on how the company measures and funds customer reliability
Track record of scaling support through automation, not headcount
indicators: Shapes the enterprise support engineering thesis
Owns the support-to-reliability strategy
Is a recognized authority in support engineering

coreCompetencies: Ownership of the enterprise strategy for support engineering and its evolution
Definition of self-service and automation as the basis of a scalable support model
Direction of the systemic reliability program that support insight feeds
Stewardship of the support data platform as a company-level reliability signal
Setting of the SLA and SLO commitments that define the customer relationship
Alignment of support strategy with product, engineering and business goals
Establishment of support engineering as a durable competitive capability
Definition of how the organization turns customer experience into product quality
complementaryCompetencies: Recognition as a leader in support engineering across the industry
Influence on support and reliability practices beyond the company
Track record of building support organizations that scale sublinearly
indicators: Leads support engineering for the whole enterprise
Defines the company's support and reliability strategy
Is a reference authority in support engineering
